reasoning: The NFCI Risk Subindex is a highly smoothed weekly series currently in a strong, multi-month downward trend (reaching -0.600 as of early December 2025). Over the past 15 weeks, the index has experienced only 1 weekly increase and 2 flat weeks, with 12 decreases. Given this strong momentum, it is highly unlikely to reverse within the span of a single week, so the probability for the first resolution date (Dec 14) is low. For the second date (Jan 6, roughly 4 weeks out), the probability of the trend reversing or ticking back up above the Dec 7 threshold increases slightly. For the third date (March 7, three months out), there is substantial potential for mean reversion or a macro cycle shift (such shifts typically take a few weeks to months to materialize, as seen in the Spring 2025 increase), making it nearly a coin toss. Our estimates reflect this increasing uncertainty and potential for mean reversion over longer horizons.
